growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Synthetic cheese-ripening medium, iron-limited (5 uM EDDHA), casein-hydrolysate N source

up.method: Multi-omics (transcriptome/proteome/metabolome) inference: in coculture H. alvei uses putrescine (synthesized by B. aurantiacum) as an energy source; not a measured flux

sec.compound: siderophore (H. alvei is the sole siderophore producer of the two)
sec.units: qualitative (CAS-assay halo positive at 5 uM EDDHA)

growth_records.cite: Mekuli, Rina et al. Iron limitation promotes metabolic cross-feeding between cheese ripening bacteria. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag100. [PMC13198182]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively only (growth curves summarized as area-under-curve, AUC; OD600); no numeric specific growth rate or doubling time given in text
growth_records.snippet: The medium was composed of 25 g/l casein or casein hydrolysate; 1.9 g/l yeast nitrogen base ...; 5 g/l glycerol; 5 g/l sodium lactate; 17 g/l sodium chloride; and 0.00004 g/l copper sulfate. It was supplemented with different levels of EDDH
growth_records.notes: Full medium composition and culture conditions stated (25 C, pH 7, 200 rpm shake flask, batch, iron-limited 5 uM EDDHA). NO numeric growth rate/doubling time or quantitative uptake/secretion flux in the paper: growth reported only as AUC of OD600 curves; cross-feeding (B. aurantiacum -> putrescine -> H. alvei energy source) inferred from multi-omics, not measured as a rate. Siderophore production is qualitative (CAS halo). Iron-sufficient comparator used 1.2 uM FeCl3 instead of 5 uM EDDHA. Record retained for medium+condition value to GEM media reconstruction.

growth_records.strain: H. alvei GB001 + B. aurantiacum 8(6), 1:1 inoculation
growth_records.medium: Synthetic cheese-ripening medium, iron-limited (5 uM EDDHA), casein N source

up.method: Multi-omics: H. alvei consumes putrescine (from B. aurantiacum) as energy source in coculture
B. aurantiacum shares H. alvei-produced siderophores, intensifying iron limitation of H. alvei

growth_records.cite: Mekuli, Rina et al. Iron limitation promotes metabolic cross-feeding between cheese ripening bacteria. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag100. [PMC13198182]
growth_records.method: reported qualitatively only (lower growth inferred from ribosomal subunit downregulation and AUC); no numeric rate
growth_records.snippet: putrescine acts as a cross-fed metabolite, where B. aurantiacum synthesizes it and H. alvei uses it as an energy source ... coculturing led to increased production of volatile sulfur compounds
growth_records.notes: Defined two-member coculture (co-culture mode), iron-limited synthetic cheese medium, full composition stated, 25 C, pH 7, 200 rpm batch. Cross-feeding: B. aurantiacum -> putrescine -> H. alvei energy source; H. alvei siderophore shared with (scavenged by) B. aurantiacum. VSC production up in coculture. All exchanges are qualitative/omics-inferred; NO numeric growth rate or quantitative flux (mmol/gDW/h) reported. Retained for medium+interaction value.
